title: Service Contract
description:
A signed (or proposed) commercial agreement between a tenant and a customer — the contract under which SKS bills the customer for maintenance work. A customer can have many service_contracts over time (initial 3-year + extensions, or a new contract on renewal); at most one is `status: active` at a time. Contract scopes (contract_scope rows) hang off a specific service_contract, not directly off the customer.

Introduced 2026-05-20 in the Door C schema split. The fields here used to live on customer.schema.json v1; they were lifted out so the customer entity could represent the CRM lifecycle (lead → prospect → active → churned) cleanly.

external_id.description: Customer-side or external system reference (PO number, contract reference).

name.description: Short label for this contract (e.g. 'Equinix AU SMCA 2024-2026', 'Jemena RCD Program 2024-2027'). Falls back to '{customer.display_name} — {term_start} to {term_end}' if null.

status.description: Lifecycle of this specific contract. draft = pre-signature; active = currently billable; expired = ran out, not renewed; superseded = replaced by a newer contract with the same customer; cancelled = terminated early.

contract_template.description: Which contract template was used. Drives derivation logic for year-fees, CPI, scope-coverage rules.

smca_agreement_number.description: SMCA / Master Services Agreement reference number.

schedule_agreement_number.description: Schedule agreement reference number (attached to a master agreement).

term_start.description: Start of the contract term. Used as Y1 base for CPI calculations.

term_end.description: End of the current contract term (before any renewal options).

options.description: Free-text renewal option encoding, e.g. '+1+1' (two one-year options).

visit_cadence.description: How often the tenant attends this customer's sites under this contract. Drives the calendar coverage check at xlsx import.

cpi_basis.description: How CPI is applied year-over-year. Equinix AU SMCA = simple_on_y1_base (Y_n = Y1*(1+rate*(n-1))) — NOT compound.

cpi_rate.description: Annual CPI rate as a decimal fraction (0.05 = 5%).

fiscal_year_basis.description: Whether contract years align to Australian FY (1 Jul - 30 Jun) or calendar year (1 Jan - 31 Dec, Equinix).

payment_terms_days.description: Days from invoice to payment due (45 = Equinix Net 45).

min_hours_after_hours.description: Minimum charge in hours for an after-hours call-out.

min_hours_weekend.description: Minimum charge in hours for a weekend call-out.

hourly_rate_effective_from.description: Date from which the current hourly rates apply.

sla_response_minutes.description: Maximum minutes to acknowledge a reactive call (15 for Equinix Premium).

sla_onsite_hours.description: Maximum hours to be on-site after a reactive call (2 for Equinix).

sla_resolution_hours.description: Maximum hours to fully resolve a reactive call (24 for Equinix).

monthly_report_due_day.description: Day of month by which the monthly performance report must be issued (Equinix = 7).

pm_reschedule_notice_days.description: Notice required (days) before rescheduling a PM visit. Equinix = 30.

service_credit_pm_breach_pct.description: Service credit as fraction of annual contract value for PM breach (0.03 = 3%).

service_credit_reactive_breach_pct.description: Service credit as fraction of annual contract value for reactive breach.

service_credit_spares_breach_pct.description: Service credit as fraction of annual contract value for spares breach.

management_hours_per_period.description: Standing supervisor/planner overhead allocation absorbed into the contract value (Equinix = 80 hrs/quarter).

smca_doc_attachment_id.description: Reference to the signed SMCA document in the attachments table.

schedule_doc_attachment_id.description: Reference to the signed schedule document in the attachments table.

supersedes_contract_id.description: When this contract replaces a previous one (renewal), points at the predecessor. Used to build a contract history per customer.

active.description: Whether this contract record is currently active (i.e. not soft-deleted). Distinct from `status=active` which is a lifecycle stage.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: term_end == null OR term_start == null OR term_end > term_start
status != 'active' OR (term_start != null AND term_end != null)
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: Contract status is active but term_start/term_end is missing.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: cpi_basis == null OR cpi_basis == 'none' OR cpi_rate != null
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: cpi_basis set but no cpi_rate provided.
management_hours_per_period set but no management_period_basis to interpret it.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: status != 'expired' OR term_end == null OR term_end < TODAY()
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: Contract status is expired but term_end is in the future — should be active.

x-eq-import-hints.description: Contracts are usually entered manually rather than bulk-imported — they're low-frequency, high-value. When bulk-imported, expect one row per (customer, term_start) pair.

common_sources.name: Contract register spreadsheet
watchouts: CPI may be '5%' or '0.05' or '5' — coerce as fraction (5% → 0.05)
Net Days often free-text 'Net 45' — coerce to integer
Customer column resolves to customer_id via FK fuzzy match against company_name / code

common_sources.name: Signed contract metadata extracted at upload time
watchouts: Set smca_doc_attachment_id / schedule_doc_attachment_id at import to keep the signed PDF linked
